h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I did complete every item on the sheet, so I thought that meant it was ready for sign-off.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that made the latch risk too simple.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects to proceed directly to running the physical test and declaring a decision based on whether the latch moves.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Before the pull, state three things aloud: the load direction, the stop condition, and the consequence if the latch opens.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to the learner's persistent reliance on the checklist in L2, the tutor physically sets the sheet aside and introduces a structured verbal protocol to shift the learner's cognitive focus to real-time risk assessment.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The condition is named correctly. Tighten one point: the trial holds if the latch slips or opens under this load. Until the test, the decision is pending.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly adopts the learner's new 'holds' framing from L3, validating their conceptual shift while refining the precision of their safety criteria.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square, but the grid is turning it into four copies.
